# Vibe Todo Constitution

## Core Principles

### I. Component-Driven Architecture

All UI MUST be built as composable Vue single-file components
(SFCs) within the Nuxt.js framework.

- Pages MUST reside in the `pages/` directory using file-based
  routing.
- Reusable UI elements MUST be extracted into `components/`.
- Shared state MUST use Vue composables in `composables/`.
- No direct DOM manipulation outside Vue's reactivity system.

### II. Retro 80s Design Consistency

The visual identity MUST maintain a consistent 80s retro
aesthetic across every screen and interaction.

- All styling MUST use Tailwind CSS utility classes; no raw CSS
  files unless extending Tailwind's configuration.
- Color palette, typography, and visual effects MUST evoke an
  80s aesthetic (neon accents, grid patterns, retro fonts).
- New UI elements MUST be reviewed for visual coherence with
  the established retro design system before merging.

### III. Technology Stack Discipline

The project MUST use only the approved technology stack. No
additional frameworks or runtime dependencies without explicit
justification and constitution amendment.

- **Framework**: Vue 3 + Nuxt.js
- **Build tool**: Vite (via Nuxt)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
- **Platform**: Web browser (SPA/SSR via Nuxt)
- Adding a dependency MUST be justified by a concrete need that
  cannot be met by the existing stack.

### IV. Simplicity First

Vibe Todo is a todo tracking application. YAGNI (You Aren't
Gonna Need It) applies strictly.

- Features MUST solve a direct user need related to todo
  management.
- Abstractions MUST NOT be introduced until a pattern repeats
  at least twice.
- Prefer Nuxt built-in capabilities (auto-imports, file-based
  routing, server routes) over custom solutions.

### V. User Experience Focus

Todo management MUST be intuitive, fast, and responsive.

- Core interactions (create, complete, delete a todo) MUST be
  achievable in one or two clicks/taps.
- The UI MUST be responsive across desktop and mobile
  viewports.
- Visual feedback MUST be provided for every user action
  (loading states, confirmations, error messages).

## Technology Stack Constraints

- **Runtime**: Node.js (LTS)
- **Package manager**: npm or pnpm (choose one and remain
  consistent throughout the project)
- **Language**: TypeScript MUST be used for all `.vue` and
  `.ts` files; plain JavaScript is not permitted.
- **Deployment**: Static generation (`nuxt generate`) or SSR
  (`nuxt build`) — decide per feature, document in plan.
- **Browser support**: Latest two versions of Chrome, Firefox,
  Safari, and Edge.

## Development Workflow

- All feature work MUST happen on a feature branch and be
  merged via pull request.
- Commits MUST follow Conventional Commits format
  (e.g., `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`).
- Linting (`eslint`) and formatting (`prettier`) MUST pass
  before a branch can be merged.
- Each pull request MUST include a description of what changed
  and how to verify it.

## Governance

This constitution is the authoritative reference for all
project decisions. When a practice conflicts with a principle
stated here, this document takes precedence.

- **Amendments**: Any change to this constitution MUST be
  proposed via pull request, reviewed, and approved before
  merging. The amendment MUST include an updated version
  number, date, and a summary of changes.
- **Versioning**: Constitution versions follow Semantic
  Versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). MAJOR for principle
  removals or redefinitions, MINOR for additions, PATCH for
  clarifications.
- **Compliance**: All pull requests and code reviews MUST
  verify adherence to the principles defined above.

**Version**: 1.0.0 | **Ratified**: 2026-04-13 | **Last Amended**: 2026-04-13

# Project Standards

<!-- Source: local .github/instructions/design-standards.instructions.md -->
# Design Standards

Follow these design system standards when working on this project:

- Use semantic HTML elements (`<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`) instead of generic `<div>` wrappers
- Follow a consistent color palette defined in design tokens
- Ensure all interactive elements have visible focus indicators
- Use responsive design patterns — mobile-first approach
- Maintain a minimum touch target size of 44x44px for interactive elements
- Follow the project's typography scale for headings and body text
